name: ELW Holy Communion, Setting Two (Ascension house form)

meta.notes: Faithful transcription of the current Sunday ordo. Block order EXACTLY follows renderer/templates/html/bulletin.html top to bottom; large_print.html and _macros.html were cross-checked for structure. Toggle names map to ServiceConfig fields (see docs/rite-engine.md). Anything that is renderer-style rather than rite structure (notation-vs-text, per-document hymn render mode, inline role prefixes/responses, per-season preface resolution) is captured in a 'note' rather than forced into the schema.

blocks.note: Composite cover page rendered from CongregationProfile (church name/address, cover image, service time, date, day name) via m.cover(). Identity/chrome, not liturgical text; profile_ref is a schema extension for profile-driven content. Does not fit the pure literal_text{text} model - see docs/rite-engine.md.
profile.welcome_message paragraph at the top of page 2.
profile.standing_instructions (the '* = please stand' note).

blocks.title: PRELUDE
blocks.note: Heading always shown; a music citation line is rendered when prelude_title is set.

blocks.title: CHORAL CALL TO WORSHIP
blocks.note: Data-driven, not a toggle: the whole section is rendered only when choral_title is set.

blocks.title: *CONFESSION AND FORGIVENESS
blocks.note: S&S may supply confession text; falls back to ELW Form A. Gated on the show_confession toggle (false for Christmas Eve).

blocks.title: *GATHERING HYMN
blocks.note: render=ref matches the congregation bulletin (title only). Actual render mode is per-document renderer-side: bulletin=title (ref), large print=lyrics, leader guide=notation image. See docs/rite-engine.md.
P/C dialog with no heading. Gated on the greeting toggle (ServiceConfig.show_greeting).

blocks.title: *KYRIE
blocks.note: Bulletin/leader-guide render the Setting Two notation image; large print renders the text dialog (text_fallback). Gated on the kyrie toggle (ServiceConfig.include_kyrie).

blocks.title: *CANTICLE OF PRAISE
blocks.note: Canticle variant 1 of 2. The single ServiceConfig.canticle enum is exposed to the condition system as two derived booleans (canticle_glory_to_god / canticle_this_is_the_feast); when canticle=none neither is true and no canticle block renders. See docs/rite-engine.md for the modeling justification.

blocks.title: *CANTICLE OF PRAISE
blocks.note: Canticle variant 2 of 2 (see canticle_glory_to_god).

blocks.title: Invitation to Lent
blocks.note: Rendered only in Lent (bulletin: is_lent). Season-gated, not toggle-gated.

blocks.title: *PRAYER OF THE DAY
blocks.note: Role heading 'Pastor'. Content from S&S (prayer_of_the_day_html); no static fallback (renders blank if absent).

blocks.title: First Reading
blocks.note: Role heading 'Scripture Reader'. Heading shows the S&S citation; the response 'The word of the Lord / Thanks be to God' is a renderer-side inline literal.

blocks.title: Psalm
blocks.note: Role heading 'The Congregation reads responsively'. Number from the S&S citation.

blocks.title: Second Reading
blocks.note: Role heading 'Scripture Reader'. Response as first_reading.

blocks.title: *GOSPEL ACCLAMATION
blocks.note: No block-level condition: the ordo always includes it and no ServiceConfig field backs a 'ga' toggle (contrary to a literal reading of the brief - see report). Bulletin renders the seasonal notation image and hides the section if the image is unavailable (renderer-side asset fallback); large print always shows the heading and falls back to the S&S acclamation text. No static text_fallback constant exists (the LP fallback is dynamic S&S text).

blocks.title: *GOSPEL
blocks.note: Role heading none. The dialog 'The holy gospel according to St. N / Glory to You, O Lord' and closing 'This is the gospel of our Lord / Praise to you, O Christ' are renderer-side inline literals.

blocks.title: *SERMON HYMN
blocks.note: render mode per-document, as gathering_hymn.
When include_baptism is true, the Holy Baptism rite REPLACES the creed (template if/else). Hence baptism is gated on baptism=true and both creed variants on baptism=false.

blocks.title: *NICENE CREED
blocks.note: Role heading 'Prayer Leader'. Creed variant 1 of 2: the ServiceConfig.creed_type enum is exposed as derived booleans creed_nicene / creed_apostles. Shown only when there is no baptism.

blocks.title: *APOSTLES CREED
blocks.note: Creed variant 2 of 2 (see creed_nicene).

blocks.title: *PRAYERS OF INTERCESSION
blocks.note: Role heading 'Prayer Leader'. Rendered as the instruction 'As each petition of the prayers ends:' plus a Leader/Congregation call-and-response; the call/response come from S&S (prayers_html) with elw.default_prayers_call / elw.default_prayers_response as defaults - resolved renderer-side, so no single fallback text_ref.

blocks.title: *SHARING OF THE PEACE
blocks.note: Role heading 'Pastor'. Inline template literals (no static_text constant exists; adding one would edit a renderer file). Large print appends a longer greeting instruction - a minor wording difference between the two templates, see report.

lines.text: The peace of Christ be with you always.
We greet one another with a sign of Christ’s peace.

blocks.title: OFFERING
blocks.note: Inline template literal leader text (no static_text constant).

lines.text: We know that all that we are and all that we have come from God. So, in response to God’s goodness, we give with cheerful hearts.

blocks.title: OFFERTORY
blocks.note: Heading switches to 'CHORAL ANTHEM' when offertory_type=choral_anthem (renderer-side variant). Music citation line rendered when offertory_title is set.

blocks.title: *HYMN
blocks.note: Fixed offertory hymn (LS 97 'Come, Lord Jesus', Tallis' Canon). Bulletin/LP render the bundled notation image if available, otherwise the verse text (text_fallback). This is a fixed liturgical hymn, not a user-chosen hymn_slot.

blocks.title: *OFFERING PRAYER
blocks.note: Role heading 'Communion Assistant'. Content from S&S; template default 'Leader: Let us pray... Amen' is a renderer-side literal, so no static fallback.

blocks.title: *GREAT THANKSGIVING
blocks.note: Role heading 'Pastor & Congregation'. Bulletin renders the notation image; large print renders the spoken P/C dialog (text_fallback).

No heading; rendered as 'Pastor (sung/spoken):' + the preface text. Per-season/per-occasion preface resolution stays renderer-side for now (ServiceConfig.preface selects the notation image in the leader guide; bulletin/LP use the short/full preface text). text_fallback anchors the full text.
Bulletin renders the notation image; large print renders the text stanzas (text_fallback).

Extended-form opening: P: first line + the 'Praise to you...' lines. ServiceConfig.eucharistic_form is exposed as the derived boolean eucharistic_extended (short form omits this block; words_of_institution below then follows the heading directly, matching the template's short branch).
Present in BOTH forms (no condition): after the praise lines in the extended form, immediately after the heading in the short form.
Inline template literal introducing the acclamation. Extended form only, and only when the memorial_acclamation toggle is on (ServiceConfig.include_memorial_acclamation).
blocks.text: With this bread and cup we remember our Lord’s passover from death to life as we proclaim the mystery of faith:

blocks.note: Single notation block. memorial_acclamation_mode selects image (sung) vs bold text (spoken) within the bulletin; large print always renders text. That mode is a renderer-side variant of the same notation, not a structural fork - see report.
Extended-form closing stanzas. 'Come, Holy Spirit' (elw.come_holy_spirit) is inserted between stanzas renderer-side.
Sung Amen notation. Bulletin (extended form) renders the image; large print has no Amen image and no text fallback, so it omits - matching the templates. Extended form only.

blocks.title: *LORD’S PRAYER
blocks.note: Preceded by the inline P: line 'Gathered into one by the Holy Spirit, let us pray as Jesus taught us.' (renderer-side literal).

blocks.title: *INVITATION TO COMMUNION
blocks.note: House text (Ascension overrides the S&S invitation). Rendered with a P: label.

blocks.title: AGNUS DEI
blocks.note: Bulletin renders the notation image; large print renders the text stanzas (text_fallback).

blocks.title: COMMUNION HYMN
blocks.note: render=notation matches the congregation bulletin (communion is the one hymn the bulletin shows as a notation image, falling back to title). Large print renders notation-or-lyrics. Data-driven: rendered only when a communion hymn is set.

blocks.text: *After all have communed, the Congregation STANDS.

blocks.note: Role 'Pastor'. Inline template literal (no static_text constant). Bulletin says 'our Lord Jesus Christ'; large print says 'our Lord Jesus' - a minor wording difference between the two templates, transcribed from bulletin.html (primary). See report.

lines.text: The body and blood of our Lord Jesus Christ strengthen you and keep you in his grace.

blocks.note: Bulletin renders the notation image; large print renders the text lines (text_fallback), preceded there by 'The Congregation SINGS:'. Gated on the nunc_dimittis toggle (ServiceConfig.show_nunc_dimittis).

blocks.title: *PRAYER AFTER COMMUNION
blocks.note: Role heading 'Communion Assistant'. Content from S&S.

blocks.title: *BLESSING
blocks.note: Role heading 'Pastor'. S&S may supply the blessing; falls back to the Aaronic blessing.
Placeholder position. Structured announcement blocks (the reserved prayer_list / week_calendar / serving_list / staff_directory / announcement_text types) arrive with their UI in LWS-7.

blocks.title: *SENDING HYMN
blocks.note: render mode per-document, as gathering_hymn.

blocks.title: SENDING
blocks.note: Role heading 'Pastor'. Rendered as a P/C dialog without the P: label. S&S may supply the dismissal; falls back to the standard dismissal (elw.dismissal, a (role,text) list).

blocks.title: *POSTLUDE
blocks.note: Music citation line rendered when postlude_title is set.
profile.copyright_paragraphs block (bulletin only; large print ends at the postlude). Profile-driven chrome - see cover.
